ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the cities table */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the states table */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date the incident occurred */,
  "dead" INTEGER /* Number of deaths in the incident */,
  "injured" INTEGER /* Number of injuries in the incident */,
  "total" INTEGER /* Total number of casualties */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "is_holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the incident occurred on a holiday */,
  "is_weekend" INTEGER /* Indicates if the incident occurred on a weekend */,
  "weekday" INTEGER /* Day of the week the incident occurred */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., mass shooting, gang violence) */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Location type (e.g., school, public place, private residence) */,
  "incident_source" TEXT /* Source of the incident report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_incident_type FOREIGN KEY ("incident_type") REFERENCES incident_types ("incident_type"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_location_type FOREIGN KEY ("location_type") REFERENCES incident_locations ("location_type")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* State code of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_code" TEXT /* State code */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., law enforcement, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the incidents table */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was submitted */,
  "report_details" TEXT /* Details of the incident report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "victims" (
  "victim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each victim */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the incidents table */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the victim */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the victim */,
  "injury_type" TEXT /* Type of injury sustained by the victim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("victim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "perpetrators" (
  "perpetrator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each perpetrator */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the incidents table */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the perpetrator */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the perpetrator */,
  "motivation" TEXT /* Motivation behind the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("perpetrator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_types" (
  "incident_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident type */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., mass shooting, gang violence) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., school, public place, private residence) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)
